summary:
Claim: Unstable angina occurs when there’s an imbalance between myocardial oxygen supply and demand.  
Evidence: - Symptoms increase in frequency or severity over time; indicates dynamic ischemia. - No evidence of necrosis (e.g., normal troponins) despite ongoing ischemia.  
Answer: C  

(497 tokens)

Note: All cases align with core features of unstable angina—progressive symptomatology and ischemia without irreversible damage—as opposed to stable angina (predictable, less frequent), MI (necrotic tissue injury), or sudden death (fatal outcome). The consistency across clinical scenarios reinforces C as the optimal fit.

Final Answer: C
